title: commersonia aspera
intro: Commersonia aspera, also known as the Rough Horse-Suede Bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia that belongs to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Commersonia aspera include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
